ac39af34-bdd6-49bc-83ce-720412f6d784.label: text_chunk
ac39af34-bdd6-49bc-83ce-720412f6d784.text:
Item No.  14.1 Halifax Regional Council August 6, 2013
TO: Mayor Savage and Members of Halifax Regional Council SUBMITTED BY: ___________________________________________________________ Richard Butts, Chief Administrative Officer __________________________________________________________ Mike Labrecque, Deputy Chief Administrative Officer DATE: July 31, 2013 SUBJECT: Non-Disclosure Agreement with the Province of Nova Scotia

ORIGIN

On June 25, 2013,

546b53b1-ec00-4e60-b89e-dd3b8396ccbf.label: text_chunk
546b53b1-ec00-4e60-b89e-dd3b8396ccbf.text:
Regional Council authorized the CAO to negotiate and execute an agreement for SAP support services with the Province of Nova Scotia.

LEGISLATIVE AUTHORITY

Under section 58(5) of the HRM Charter, Regional Council has the power to make and carry out a  contract.

5d9e776e-605a-4fea-9d56-8c23c00b6653.label: text_chunk
5d9e776e-605a-4fea-9d56-8c23c00b6653.text: Under  section  10(3)  of  the  HRM  Charter,  the  Mayor  and  Clerk  may  sign  any document to which the Municipality is a party on behalf of the Municipality.

618a48dd-1fd8-45d4-b15d-1d80042739d4.label: text_chunk
618a48dd-1fd8-45d4-b15d-1d80042739d4.text:
RECOMMENDATION

It  is  recommended  that  Regional  Council  authorize  the  Mayor  and  Clerk  to  sign  a  Non- Disclosure Agreement with the Province of Nova Scotia with terms and conditions substantially similar to the draft agreement attached hereto as Attachment “A”.Non-Disclosure Agreement with the Province of NS - 2 - Council Report
August 6, 2013

BACKGROUND

In furtherance of the June 25,

21736334-2903-43be-8cd1-9120cd3e735d.label: text_chunk
21736334-2903-43be-8cd1-9120cd3e735d.text:
2013 directive of Regional Council, staff entered into negotiations with the Province of Nova Scotia  (“PNS”) on those terms and conditions that were not  already agreed to during transition planning.

DISCUSSION

It became apparent during these negotiations that HRM staff needed to be made aware of certain key terms of the PNS’ agreement with its SAP service provider in order to understand and be in a position to negotiate for appropriate service level standards.

d79e8381-6c96-419d-85d2-8bc7d1604240.label: text_chunk
d79e8381-6c96-419d-85d2-8bc7d1604240.text:
The PNS has taken the position that it  cannot  disclose  this  and  other  information  to  HRM  unless  HRM  and  the  PNS  enter  into  a mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement.

On July 26, 2013, the PNS forwarded a proposed Non-Disclosure Agreement to staff.

e0d1c0df-18eb-45e0-9b0c-534e8561f8a2.label: text_chunk
e0d1c0df-18eb-45e0-9b0c-534e8561f8a2.text: This Non- Disclosure  Agreement  permits  the  PNS  and  HRM  to  disclose  confidential  information  to  each other during the period of negotiations.

3df1370e-6645-4908-b3e7-555b8ccdb2e4.label: text_chunk
3df1370e-6645-4908-b3e7-555b8ccdb2e4.text: Any confidential information exchanged between HRM and the PNS “shall only be used for the purposes of facilitating the business relationship between the Parties” and once the agreement terminates each party must “promptly return or destroy” all confidential information received from the other party.It is the intention of HRM and the PNS to execute an agreement for the provision of SAP support services  before  September  9,  2013.

ad592b32-ebcb-4759-bd7f-1a821e420250.label: text_chunk
ad592b32-ebcb-4759-bd7f-1a821e420250.text: As  a  result,  staff  is  seeking  direction  on  this  matter  from Regional  Council  on  August  6,  2013,  rather  than  waiting  for  the  next  scheduled  meeting  of Regional Council on September 10, 2013.

57ee4db0-ddf7-46a6-b679-5fe2a68975d5.label: text_chunk
57ee4db0-ddf7-46a6-b679-5fe2a68975d5.text:
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS

N/A

COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT

N/A

ENVIRONMENTAL IMPLICATIONS

N/A
Non-Disclosure Agreement with the Province of NS - 3 - Council Report
August 6, 2013

ALTERNATIVES

Regional  Council  could  decline  to  authorize  execution  of  the  Non-Disclosure  Agreement,  in which  case  staff  will,

d323325d-b801-4a2c-b8fe-c5d6fa2e16d7.label: text_chunk
d323325d-b801-4a2c-b8fe-c5d6fa2e16d7.text: in  accordance  with  the  June  25,  2013  directive  of  Regional  Council, request  that the  PNS  continue to  negotiate with  HRM  with  the goal  of executing an agreement for  SAP  support  services,  notwithstanding  HRM’s  refusal  to  sign  the  Non-Disclosure Agreement.

6d8b028c-9fcf-4bca-a600-06be515d542c.label: text_chunk
6d8b028c-9fcf-4bca-a600-06be515d542c.text: If  negotiations  are  permitted  to  continue  despite  HRM’s  refusal  to  sign  the  Non-Disclosure Agreement, it is  anticipated that the  PNS will refuse to  provide HRM  with  certain  confidential information  that  might  assist  HRM  in  formulating  and  pursuing  an  appropriate  negotiation position.

64e5fe46-7cb1-4986-8360-ea4c2b76f052.label: text_chunk
64e5fe46-7cb1-4986-8360-ea4c2b76f052.text:
ATTACHMENTS

Attachment “A”: Draft Non-Disclosure Agreement

c4c94e9b-a566-4494-8e05-be3a853e5736.label: text_chunk
c4c94e9b-a566-4494-8e05-be3a853e5736.text:
Report Prepared by: David Greener, Senior Solicitor, Legal Services, 490-3960 Report Approved by: ________________________________________________ Martin Ward, Q.C., Acting Director, Legal Services, 490-3960 ______________________________________________________________________________
MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE/CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT effective the day of , 2013.

BETWEEN:

HER MAJESTY THE QUEEN in right of the Province of Nova Scotia,

c11eaddf-1687-4bdc-8dde-58310315160f.label: text_chunk
c11eaddf-1687-4bdc-8dde-58310315160f.text:
as represented by the Minister of Finance
OF THE FIRST PART -and- HALIFAX REGIONAL MUNICIPALITY,

654ff489-63cc-4dff-9fb0-6e0392f14bb6.label: text_chunk
654ff489-63cc-4dff-9fb0-6e0392f14bb6.text:
as represented by the Chief Administrative Officer

OF THE SECOND PART

WHEREAS the  Province  of  Nova  Scotia (the  “Province”)  and  Halifax  Regional  Municipality  (the  “HRM”) hereinafter referred  to as  the  “Receiving  or  Disclosing  Party”  or “Parties”,

b7d2536c-57a9-4b48-8052-2fe216b3d58a.label: text_chunk
b7d2536c-57a9-4b48-8052-2fe216b3d58a.text: may  be facilitating  the potential transaction  of  business  related  to  the  provision  of  SAP  Services  by  the  Province  to  the  HRM  where  each Party may disclose certain confidential information to each other.

11a230b4-c375-40d6-89e5-dc1032fa1a3d.label: text_chunk
11a230b4-c375-40d6-89e5-dc1032fa1a3d.text:
AND  WHEREAS each  Party  wishes  to  ensure  that  the  other  treats  this  information  in  the  strictest confidence.NOW  THEREFORE in  consideration  of  the  promises  and  mutual  covenants  contained  herein,  and  other good and valuable consideration, the receipt of which is hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

1.  The  confidential,

64c2c222-4ce1-4278-8cec-597e11be4164.label: text_chunk
64c2c222-4ce1-4278-8cec-597e11be4164.text: proprietary  and  trade  secret  information  of  the  Disclosing  Party  (hereinafter "Confidential Information") provided hereunder is any and all information, in whatever form (including electronic data) that will be disclosed to facilitate the potential transaction of business (related to the provision  of  SAP  Services  to  HRM)  between  the  Parties,  including,  but  not  limited  to,

4bc91c39-6447-4adb-84d6-c8d7587eef55.label: text_chunk
4bc91c39-6447-4adb-84d6-c8d7587eef55.text: portions  or parts of the November 1, 2012 Agreement (including any schedules  or appendices) between the Province of Nova Scotia and IBM, each Party's disclosure of intellectual property, techniques, sketches,  drawings,  models,  inventions,  know-how,  processes,  apparatus,  equipment,  algorithms, software  programs,  software  source  documents,  and  formulae  related  to  the  current,

f37fcba4-ed35-4485-9566-e6ae59b4a394.label: text_chunk
f37fcba4-ed35-4485-9566-e6ae59b4a394.text: future  and proposed  products,  documents  and  services,  research,  experimental  work,  development,  design details and specifications, engineering, financial information, procurement requirements, purchasing, manufacturing,  licensing,  sales  or  service  customer  lists,  business  forecasts,  sales  and merchandizing, and marketing plans and information.

e4a09017-4952-4bf2-9556-63517982e9c3.label: text_chunk
e4a09017-4952-4bf2-9556-63517982e9c3.text:
For purposes of this Agreement confidential, proprietary  and  trade  secret  information  of the  Province’s  SAP  Service  Provider  or  any  other Province’s  Service  Provider, any  parent  company,  subsidiary  or  affiliate  of  the  Disclosing  party shall be considered Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party for purposes of this Agreement.

2.  The term Confidential Information shall not include information,

7773b595-6a8b-437f-9a39-449fd3edb984.label: text_chunk

6750a4e2-2391-493e-8d41-76bf608b2d39.label: text_chunk
6750a4e2-2391-493e-8d41-76bf608b2d39.text:
or

(e) generally  made  available  to  third  parties  by  the  Disclosing  Party  without  restriction  on disclosure.

3.

  The  Receiving  Party  agrees  that  Confidential  Information  shall  be  used  only  for  the  purposes  of facilitating  the  business  relationship  between  the  Parties.

882dd04a-0f0a-4b92-93f3-74e055bf6484.label: text_chunk
882dd04a-0f0a-4b92-93f3-74e055bf6484.text: Additionally,  subject  to  section  4  below, the  Receiving  Party  shall  not  disclose  Confidential  Information  to  any  third  party  without  the  prior written  approval  of  the  Disclosing  Party.

0be7fc2f-514b-4b8c-a42b-45e99742dad5.label: text_chunk
0be7fc2f-514b-4b8c-a42b-45e99742dad5.text: The  Receiving  Party  shall  maintain  the  Confidential Information  with  at  least  the  same  degree  of  care  that  the  Receiving  Party  uses  to  protect  its  own similar categories of confidential and proprietary information, but no less than a reasonable degree of  care  under  the  circumstances.

282cf129-7817-4ba1-a4aa-2ea47db0df1d.label: text_chunk
282cf129-7817-4ba1-a4aa-2ea47db0df1d.text:
The  Receiving  Party  shall  not  make  any  copies  of  Confidential Information  except  as  necessary  to  perform  hereunder.    Each  Party  shall  be  responsible  for  any breach  of  this  Agreement  by  such  Party,  its  employees,  agents,  officials,  representatives  or consultants acting within the scope of their engagement or employment.

4.

289611cb-c6ed-477d-842d-3179ef4b7a94.label: text_chunk
289611cb-c6ed-477d-842d-3179ef4b7a94.text:
The  HRM  acknowledges  and  agrees  that  the  Province  may  disclose  the  Confidential  Information received  from  the  HRM  to  its  external  or  internal auditors,  as  well  as  the  Province’s  SAP  Service Provider or Province’s other service providers for the purpose of provision or extension of the SAP Services to HRM.

5.

38a60c7b-c588-4742-93d1-2978b18d4e23.label: text_chunk
38a60c7b-c588-4742-93d1-2978b18d4e23.text: The  Receiving  Party  will  adhere  to  all  Canadian  export  laws,  rules  and  regulations,  and  will  not export  or  re-export  any  technical  data  or  products  received  from  the  Disclosing  Party,  nor  will  the receiving party export or re-export the associated products of such technical data, to any restricted country and/or to any company,

089dda50-3ec2-444e-92ab-fe942eef6a5a.label: text_chunk
089dda50-3ec2-444e-92ab-fe942eef6a5a.text:
individual or government entity listed in the Table of Denial Orders published from time to time by the Canadian Government (called denied persons).

6.  This Agreement will be effective as of the date of this Agreement,

 but will apply to any Confidential Information  disclosed  to  the  Receiving  Party  from  the  Disclosing  Party  prior  to  such  date  and  will continue in perpetuity.

7.

e9464842-28b6-4508-9d6f-59c5f140f59c.label: text_chunk
e9464842-28b6-4508-9d6f-59c5f140f59c.text: Each Party agrees that the harm resulting from improper disclosure of Confidential Information may be irreparable; therefore, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other equitable relief  to  prevent  or  restrain  breaches  of  any  of  the  provisions  of  this  Agreement  by  the  Receiving Party  or  any  of  its  employees,  agents,  officials,  representatives  or  consultants,

23d4b567-5928-4e2a-b30c-8ce845076759.label: text_chunk
23d4b567-5928-4e2a-b30c-8ce845076759.text:
or  to  enforce  the terms  and  provisions  hereof,  by  an  action  instituted  in  a  court  of  competent  jurisdiction,  which remedy  or  remedies  are  in  addition  to  and  not  in  substitution  for  any  other  remedy  to  which  the Disclosing Party may be entitled at law or in equity.

8.

c3a0cb68-e9d3-412a-875b-cf74b3788549.label: text_chunk
c3a0cb68-e9d3-412a-875b-cf74b3788549.text:
Title to, interest in,

 and all other rights of ownership to Confidential Information shall remain with the Disclosing Party. The information provided hereunder, including Confidential Information, is provided
3
strictly "as is" and without representation and warranty of any kind as to its accuracy, completeness, freedom from error, value, merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose or non-infringement.

9.

e55fbb79-5ef4-4fcd-928e-8f1df0098548.label: text_chunk
e55fbb79-5ef4-4fcd-928e-8f1df0098548.text: This  Agreement  will  terminate  on  the  date  and  time  the  Agreement  between  the  Parties  over  the provision  of  SAP  Services  is  terminated.

44378b47-b922-4e85-8562-6fee56a3cfa9.label: text_chunk
44378b47-b922-4e85-8562-6fee56a3cfa9.text:
In  the  event  this  Agreement  is  terminated,  and  the Disclosing  Party  so  requests,  the  Receiving  Party  shall  promptly  return  or  destroy  (and  certify destruction of) all Confidential Information which it received from the  Disclosing Party along with all copies.

  The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement or the return of the Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party.

a9715512-b3a7-403d-aa5f-ee73c4e5d078.label: text_chunk
a9715512-b3a7-403d-aa5f-ee73c4e5d078.text:
10.

 The  Parties  acknowledge  and  understand  that  all  documentation  and  information,  including Confidential  Information,  provided  to  the  Province  of  Nova  Scotia  or  HRM,  as  the  case  may  be, pursuant  to  this  Agreement  shall  be  kept  confidential,

96a4f949-8d32-4acd-8074-bf29110b344c.label: text_chunk
96a4f949-8d32-4acd-8074-bf29110b344c.text:
subject  to  the  provisions  of  the Freedom  of Information  and  Protection  of  Privacy  Act ,  Province  of  Nova  Scotia,  and  such  other  laws  of  the Province of Nova Scotia and Canada which may apply from time to time.

11.

b5b0d2e8-00b8-4a33-aae2-2c2cfc80772b.label: text_chunk
b5b0d2e8-00b8-4a33-aae2-2c2cfc80772b.text:
This  Agreement  shall  not  be  construed  as  creating  a  joint  venture,  partnership  or  other  form  of business  association  between  the  Parties,  nor  an  obligation  to  buy  or  sell  products  using  or incorporating  the  Confidential  Information,  nor  to  create  an  implied  or  express  license  from  either Party to the other.

12.

6088fee1-1ec0-4c29-a5e1-611ebaf6c625.label: text_chunk
6088fee1-1ec0-4c29-a5e1-611ebaf6c625.text:
The  failure  of  either  Party  to  enforce  any  right  resulting  from  breach  of  any  provision  of  this Agreement  by  the  other  Party  shall  not  be  deemed  a  waiver  of  any  right  relating  to  a  subsequent breach of such provision or of any other right hereunder.

13.

19f50a3d-ba42-414a-8b82-722664920448.label: text_chunk
19f50a3d-ba42-414a-8b82-722664920448.text:
This Agreement shall be governed by,

 and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Nova Scotia and the laws of Canada applicable therein.

14. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement, written or verbal,

 between the parties with respect to  the  terms  set  forth  herein.    This  Agreement  may  not  be  amended  except  in  writing  signed  by  a duly authorized representative of the respective parties.

[The space below is intentionally left blank.]

8984d831-4379-4baa-bb7c-6997aa6128e2.label: text_chunk
8984d831-4379-4baa-bb7c-6997aa6128e2.text:
4
15. Time shall be of the essence in this Agreement.IN WITNESS WHEREOF the parties hereto have caused this Agreement to be executed by its duly authorized officers as of the day and year first above written.

The relationships outlined involve a legal agreement between entities referred to as "Party" and "Parties." 

1. **Involvement in Legal Agreement**: The entity "Party" is part of the "Parties" involved in a legal agreement, indicating a shared contractual relationship.

2. **Binding Agreement**: Each "Party" is bound by the terms of the "Agreement," which delineates their responsibilities and actions concerning Confidential Information.

3. **Breach of Agreement**: The relationship between the "Party" and the "Agreement" is significant as it highlights that both parties are responsible for enforcing their rights and obligations. A breach by one party can adversely affect the rights of the other party, emphasizing the interconnectedness of their legal responsibilities.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the collaborative and binding nature of the contractual obligations between the involved parties, as well as the implications of any breaches that may occur.

The relationships outlined involve a central entity known as the Disclosing Party, which interacts with both the Receiving Party and the Province, as well as the Agreement governing their interactions. 

1. **Disclosing Party and Receiving Party**: The Disclosing Party provides confidential information to the Receiving Party, which is crucial for their business relationship. This indicates a trust-based exchange where sensitive information is shared to facilitate collaboration.

2. **Disclosing Party and Province**: The Disclosing Party also provides services to the Province, suggesting that it operates within this geographical area and has a business relationship with the local government or community.

3. **Disclosing Party and Agreement**: The Disclosing Party is entitled to seek injunctive relief as outlined in the Agreement, particularly in cases where the Receiving Party breaches the terms. This highlights the legal protections in place for the Disclosing Party regarding the handling of confidential information.

4. **Disclosing Party and Confidential Information**: The Disclosing Party owns the rights to the Confidential Information it shares, establishing a clear ownership and control over the sensitive data involved in their business dealings.

Overall, the Disclosing Party plays a pivotal role in providing essential services and information while maintaining legal rights and protections in its relationships with both the Receiving Party and the Province.

1. **Confidentiality Agreement**: The Receiving Party and Disclosing Party are bound by a confidentiality agreement that specifies the terms for sharing confidential information and the Receiving Party's obligations to protect that information.

2. **Use of Information**: The Receiving Party agrees to utilize the confidential information provided by the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of facilitating their business relationship, ensuring that the information is not misused.

3. **Maintenance of Confidentiality**: The Receiving Party is tasked with maintaining the confidentiality of the information it receives, highlighting its responsibility to safeguard sensitive data.

4. **Exchange of Goods and Information**: The Receiving Party receives technical data or products from the Disclosing Party, establishing a direct relationship that involves the exchange of valuable information and goods.

5. **Compliance with Export Laws**: The Receiving Party is required to adhere to Canadian export laws, which regulate its handling of the technical data and products received from the Disclosing Party.

Overall, these relationships emphasize the importance of confidentiality, responsible use of shared information, and legal compliance in the context of business interactions between the Receiving Party and the Disclosing Party.

2. **Governance of Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM)**: The Province of Nova Scotia exercises governance over the Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM), providing oversight and support as part of its jurisdictional responsibilities. community_summary.35:
The relationships outlined highlight the City’s proactive approach to managing infrastructure and securing funding for various projects. 

1. **Funding Relationships**: The City receives financial support from Measure W, which is utilized to enhance its projects. Additionally, the City actively applies for funding from the State, particularly through programs like the State Revolving Fund, to bolster its financial resources.

2. **Project Management**: The City is responsible for managing the Marie Canyon Green Streets project, indicating its role in overseeing urban development initiatives as part of its Capital Improvement Program. Furthermore, the City oversees the City Hall Roof Replacement project, showcasing its commitment to maintaining local infrastructure.

3. **Collaboration for Improvement**: The City collaborates with FEMA to develop a storm drain inventory, leveraging federal funding to improve infrastructure in response to challenges posed by events such as the Woolsey Fire.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City’s strategic efforts in funding acquisition, project management, and collaboration with federal agencies to enhance community infrastructure and resilience.
community_summary.36:
The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure** and **Los Angeles County Flood Control**: The Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure project is dependent on obtaining a maintenance agreement from the Los Angeles County Flood Control organization, which is essential for the project's construction to move forward.

2. **Regulation 070** and **vehicle impact protection devices**: Regulation 070 stipulates the necessity for installing vehicle impact protection devices. This regulation aims to enhance safety in areas that are close to outdoor pedestrian seating, ensuring the protection of pedestrians in these environments. 

Overall, these relationships highlight the importance of regulatory compliance and organizational agreements in the development and safety measures of urban infrastructure projects.

4. **Malibu Park Drainage Improvements** and **Birdview Avenue Improvements**: The inclusion of Birdview Avenue Improvements in the Malibu Park Drainage Improvements project further emphasizes the interconnectedness of these projects.

Overall, the summary illustrates how specific repair and improvement projects are geographically and functionally linked, particularly focusing on the areas of Malibu Road and Birdview Avenue within the broader context of drainage improvements in Malibu Park.

community_summary.39:
The summary of relationships involving the City is as follows:

1. **Disaster Recovery Projects**: The City manages Disaster Recovery Projects aimed at restoring and improving services and infrastructure following disasters, highlighting its commitment to community resilience.

2. **Collaboration with Caltrans**: The City has engaged in discussions with Caltrans regarding project phasing, showcasing a collaborative effort in managing transportation-related projects.

3. **Evaluation of Construction Bids**: The City received and evaluated construction bids for a project but ultimately rejected them due to a budget shortfall, illustrating its active role in the procurement process and fiscal responsibility.

4. **Collaboration with the Planning Department**: The City is working alongside the Planning Department to secure CEQA clearance for various projects, indicating a cooperative approach to urban planning and regulatory compliance.

5. **Contract with Kimley Horn**: The City has formalized a contract with Kimley Horn to conduct a traffic study, reflecting its initiative to enhance traffic management through expert analysis.

6. **Traffic Signals Infrastructure**: The City maintains traffic signals as part of its infrastructure, which are essential for managing vehicular flow and ensuring public safety on the roads.

Overall, these relationships demonstrate the City's proactive management of infrastructure, collaboration with key agencies, and commitment to effective urban planning and disaster recovery.

2. The **Malibu Park Resurfacing Project** encompasses **Busch Drive**, emphasizing the importance of this road within the overall project aimed at upgrading park facilities and access.

3. The **Citywide Asphalt Concrete Berms Repairs** project includes a critical **Design** phase, which is essential for planning and executing the necessary repairs before construction can commence.

4. The **Storm Drain Master Plan** is part of the larger **Capital Improvement and Disaster Recovery Projects**, signifying its role in enhancing the community's infrastructure resilience and disaster preparedness.

Overall, these relationships illustrate a comprehensive approach to urban development, focusing on infrastructure improvements that enhance both functionality and community well-being.
community_summary.43:
The relationships outlined involve key entities such as the Public Works and Public Safety Commission, the City Council, and the City, focusing on the approval processes for projects.

1. **Public Works and Public Safety Commission -> City Council**: The commission is presenting a project to the City Council for direction and approval, highlighting a formal relationship where the commission seeks the council's endorsement for its initiatives.

2. **PCH Signal Synchronization System Improvements Project -> City**: This project necessitates material approvals from the City, establishing a direct relationship where the City holds the authority to grant the necessary permissions for the project's advancement.

Overall, these relationships underscore the collaborative framework between the Public Works and Public Safety Commission and the City Council, as well as the critical role of the City in approving significant projects like the PCH Signal Synchronization System Improvements.
community_summary.44: The Westward Beach Road Drainage Improvements Project is currently focused on obtaining a Coastal Development Permit (CDP) application. This application is essential for securing the necessary permits to advance construction activities in coastal regions. The relationship emphasizes the project's dependency on the CDP application to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements for development in sensitive coastal areas.
community_summary.45:
The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **FEMA and CalOES**: These two entities collaborate to approve disaster recovery projects, such as the Latigo Canyon Road Retaining Wall Repair Project. Their partnership ensures that federal and state resources are effectively aligned for emergency management.

2. **Civic Center Water Treatment Facility and State Water Board**: The Civic Center Water Treatment Facility is engaged in collaboration with the State Water Board to review cultural resources as part of its funding application process. This partnership is crucial for ensuring compliance and thorough evaluation during the funding application.

3. **Civic Center Water Treatment Facility and Federal Funding**: The Civic Center Water Treatment Facility has submitted a request for federal funding to support its project, highlighting its initiative to secure financial resources for development and improvement efforts.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the importance of collaboration between governmental entities and the proactive steps taken by facilities to secure funding and ensure effective project management.
community_summary.46: The Clover Heights Storm Drainage Improvements project is actively engaged in a relationship with CalOES, as it has submitted plans for review to the agency. This indicates a direct and significant interaction where the project is seeking approval from CalOES, highlighting the importance of regulatory oversight in the development of the drainage improvements. The relationship underscores the project's reliance on agency approval to move forward.
community_summary.47:
The relationships outlined highlight the regulatory and operational dependencies between various entities:

1. **Planning Department and CEQA**: The Planning Department is required to comply with the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) regulations when reviewing development projects. This establishes a critical regulatory relationship that ensures environmental considerations are integrated into planning processes.

2. **Traffic Signals and Backup Power System**: Traffic signals necessitate a backup power system to ensure they remain operational during power outages. This relationship emphasizes the importance of maintaining traffic safety and functionality, highlighting the need for reliable infrastructure in urban planning.

In summary, the Planning Department's adherence to CEQA regulations is essential for responsible development oversight, while the requirement for traffic signals to have a backup power system underscores the importance of continuous operation for public safety.
community_summary.48: The Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs project was cancelled due to the inability to secure approval from FEMA. This highlights a significant relationship where the project's status is directly influenced by FEMA's decision-making process.
community_summary.49: The Birdview Avenue Improvements project is overseen by CalOES, establishing a direct management relationship between the project and the California Office of Emergency Services (CalOES). This indicates that CalOES is responsible for the administration and execution of the improvements on Birdview Avenue.
community_summary.50: In this relationship summary, the key entities involved are the Project and the Council. The relationship indicates that the Council formally accepted the Project, signifying an endorsement or approval from this governing body. This approval is crucial as it reflects the Council's support and validation of the Project's objectives and plans.
community_summary.51:
The relationships involving the City highlight its multifaceted role in managing public infrastructure and safety initiatives. 

1. **Capital Improvements Projects**: The City manages and oversees various Capital Improvements Projects aimed at enhancing public infrastructure, demonstrating its commitment to community development.

2. **Staff Employment**: The City employs staff who are integral to its operations, indicating a structured municipal organization that relies on its workforce to implement various projects and services.

3. **FEMA Hazard Mitigation Grant**: The City has been awarded a FEMA Hazard Mitigation grant, which signifies its role as a recipient of federal funding aimed at improving disaster resilience and safety measures.

4. **Emergency Warning Sirens**: The City oversees the implementation of the Emergency Warning Sirens project, which includes managing project activities and applying for future funding to ensure the system's effectiveness in public safety.

Overall, these relationships underscore the City's responsibilities in infrastructure management, staff employment, disaster preparedness, and public safety initiatives.
community_summary.52:
The relationships outlined involve the entity "Construction Bids" and its connection to the timeline of construction activities. 

1. **Construction Bids** are set to **initiate** the commencement of construction, which is expected to begin in **Fall 2022**. This indicates that the bidding process is a precursor to the actual construction work.

2. The **Scheduled To Begin** phase is linked to **Construction Bids** in **Spring 2022**, highlighting that bids will be solicited during this period in preparation for the construction that is anticipated to start in Fall 2022.

In summary, the construction bidding process is a critical step that will lead to the start of construction in Fall 2022, with bids being actively solicited in Spring 2022 to facilitate this timeline.
community_summary.53: The CalOES Project is a significant initiative focused on infrastructure improvements, specifically involving the repairs to Westward Beach Road. The project has a defined timeline, with the completion of the design phase for the Malibu Road slope repairs scheduled for Summer 2022. This timeline underscores the project's progress and its connection to the specific location of Westward Beach Road, highlighting the importance of these repairs in enhancing the area's infrastructure.
community_summary.54: The Point Dume Decomposed Granite Walkway Repair Project is directly linked to the Woolsey Fire, which caused significant damage to the sidewalk sections. This event necessitated the initiation of the repair project to restore the affected areas. The relationship highlights the impact of the Woolsey Fire on local infrastructure, emphasizing the need for subsequent repair efforts.
